You shall always do this, just as I have done—just as I have broken bread, blessed it, and given it to you.

You shall do this in remembrance of my body, which I have shown to you. It will be a testimony to the Father that you always remember me. And if you always remember me, you will have my Spirit to be with you.
When he had said these words, he commanded his disciples to take the wine from the cup, drink of it, and give it to the crowd so they could drink also.
They did so—they drank and were filled. Then they gave it to the crowd, and they drank and were filled.

When the disciples had done this, Jesus said to them: Blessed are you for doing this, for it fulfills my commandments. It is a witness to the Father that you are willing to do what I have commanded you.

You shall always do this for those who repent and are baptized in my name. You shall do it in remembrance of my blood, which I have shed for you, so that you may witness to the Father that you always remember me. If you always remember me, you will have my Spirit to be with you.
I give you a commandment to do these things. If you always do these things, blessed are you, for you are built upon my rock.

But whoever does more or less than these things is not built upon my rock, but on a sandy foundation. When the rain falls, the floods come, and the winds blow and beat against them, they will fall, and the gates of hell stand ready to receive them.
Therefore, blessed are you if you keep my commandments, which the Father has commanded me to give you.
Truly I say to you: You must watch and pray always, or you will be tempted by the devil and led away as his captive.
Just as I have prayed among you, so shall you pray in my church, among my people who repent and are baptized in my name. I am the light. I have set an example for you.
When Jesus had spoken these words to his disciples, he turned again to the crowd and said to them:
Truly I say to you: You must watch and pray always, so that you do not fall into temptation, for Satan wants to have you so he can sift you like wheat.
Therefore, you must always pray to the Father in my name.
Whatever you ask the Father in my name that is right, if you believe you will receive it, it will be given to you.
Pray together in your families to the Father, always in my name, so that your wives and children may be blessed.
You shall meet together often, and you shall not turn anyone away from your gatherings. Allow them to come and do not forbid them.
Pray for them, and do not cast them out. If they come often, pray for them to the Father in my name.
Therefore, hold up your light so it may shine to the world. I am the light you shall hold up—what you have seen me do. You see that I have prayed to the Father, and you all have witnessed it.

You see that I have not told any of you to go away, but rather have invited you to come to me, so that you could feel and see. In the same way you shall do for the world. Whoever breaks this commandment allows himself to be led into temptation.
When Jesus had spoken these words, he turned his attention again to the disciples he had chosen and said to them:
Truly I say to you, I give you one more commandment, and then I must go to my Father to fulfill other commandments he has given me.
This is the commandment I give you: You shall not knowingly allow anyone unworthy to partake of my flesh and blood when you administer it.

For whoever eats and drinks my flesh and blood unworthily brings condemnation upon their own soul. Therefore, if you know that a person is unworthy to eat and drink of my flesh and blood, you shall forbid them.

Nevertheless, you shall not cast that person out from among you. Instead, you shall serve them and pray for them to the Father in my name. If they repent and are baptized in my name, then you shall welcome them and share with them my flesh and blood.
But if they do not repent, they shall not be numbered among my people, so that they do not destroy my people. For I know my sheep, and they are numbered.

Nevertheless, you shall not cast them out of your places of worship, for you shall continue to minister to them. You do not know but that they may return and repent and come to me with all their heart, and I will heal them. You shall be the means of bringing salvation to them.
Therefore, keep these instructions I have given you, so that you do not come under condemnation—for woe to the one whom the Father condemns.
I give you these commandments because of the disagreements that have been among you. Blessed are you if you have no disagreements among you.
Now I am going to the Father, because it is necessary for me to go to the Father for your sake.
When Jesus had finished saying these things, he touched the disciples he had chosen, one by one, until he had touched them all. He spoke to them as he touched them.

The crowd did not hear the words he spoke, so they could not testify of them. But the disciples testified that he gave them power to give the Holy Ghost. I will show you later that this record is true.
When Jesus had touched them all, a cloud came and overshadowed the crowd so they could not see Jesus.
While they were covered by the cloud, he departed from them and ascended into heaven. The disciples saw this and testified that he ascended into heaven.

19.text: When Jesus had ascended into heaven, the crowd went home. Every man took his wife and children and returned to his own house.
Word spread immediately among the people, before it was even dark, that the crowd had seen Jesus, that he had ministered to them, and that he would show himself again to the crowd the next day.

All through the night, word spread about Jesus. So many people heard the news that a very large number worked hard all through the night to reach the place where Jesus would appear to the crowd the next day.

The next morning, when the crowd had gathered, Nephi and his brother Timothy (whom he had raised from the dead), and also his son Jonas, and Mathoni, and Mathonihah his brother, and Kumen, and Kumenonhi, and Jeremiah, and Shemnon, and Jonas, and Zedekiah, and Isaiah—these were the names of the disciples Jesus had chosen—went forward and stood in the middle of the crowd.
The crowd was so large that they divided them into twelve groups.
The twelve taught the crowd, and they had the people kneel down and pray to the Father in the name of Jesus.
The disciples also prayed to the Father in the name of Jesus. Then they stood up and ministered to the people.
They taught the same words Jesus had spoken—not changing anything from what Jesus had said. Then they knelt again and prayed to the Father in the name of Jesus.
They prayed for what they desired most, and what they desired was that the Holy Ghost would be given to them.
When they had prayed, they went down to the water's edge, and the crowd followed them.
Nephi went down into the water and was baptized.
He came up out of the water and began to baptize. He baptized all those whom Jesus had chosen.
When they had all been baptized and had come up out of the water, the Holy Ghost fell upon them, and they were filled with the Holy Ghost and with fire.
They were encircled about as if by fire. It came down from heaven, and the crowd witnessed it and testified of it. Angels came down from heaven and ministered to them.
While the angels were ministering to the disciples, Jesus came and stood among them and ministered to them.
He spoke to the crowd and commanded them to kneel down upon the earth again, and he commanded his disciples to kneel down as well.
When they had all knelt down, he commanded his disciples to pray.
They began to pray, and they prayed to Jesus, calling him their Lord and their God.
Jesus stepped away from them a short distance, bowed himself to the earth, and said:
Father, I thank thee that thou hast given the Holy Ghost to these whom I have chosen. It is because of their belief in me that I have chosen them out of the world.
Father, I pray that thou wilt give the Holy Ghost to all who will believe in their words.
Father, thou hast given them the Holy Ghost because they believe in me. Thou seest that they believe in me because thou hearest them—they pray to me, and they pray to me because I am with them.
Now, Father, I pray for them, and also for all those who will believe in their words, that they may believe in me, that I may be in them as thou, Father, art in me, so that we may be one.

When Jesus had prayed this to the Father, he came to his disciples. They were still praying to him without stopping. They did not use many words, for they were given what to pray, and they were filled with desire.

Jesus blessed them as they prayed to him. His face smiled upon them, and the light of his face shone upon them. They became as white as the face and clothing of Jesus—and the whiteness exceeded anything else on earth. Nothing on earth could be as white.
Jesus said to them: Pray on. Yet they did not stop praying.
He turned from them again, went a short distance away, and bowed himself to the earth. He prayed again to the Father, saying:

Father, I thank thee that thou hast purified those whom I have chosen because of their faith. I pray for them and also for those who will believe in their words, that they may be purified in me through faith in their words, even as they are purified in me.

Father, I pray not for the world, but for those whom thou hast given me out of the world because of their faith, that they may be purified in me, that I may be in them as thou, Father, art in me, that we may be one, that I may be glorified in them.
When Jesus had spoken these words, he came again to his disciples. They were still praying steadily, without stopping, to him. He smiled upon them again, and they were white, even as Jesus.
He went a short distance away again and prayed to the Father.
No tongue can speak the words he prayed, and no person can write the words he prayed.
The crowd heard him and testified of it. Their hearts were opened, and they understood in their hearts the words he prayed.
Nevertheless, the words he prayed were so great and marvelous that they cannot be written or spoken by any person.

When Jesus had finished praying, he came again to the disciples and said to them: I have never seen such great faith among all the Jews. Because of their unbelief, I could not show them such great miracles.
Truly I say to you, none of them have seen such great things as you have seen, and none of them have heard such great things as you have heard.

20.text: He commanded the crowd to stop praying aloud, and his disciples also. But he commanded them not to stop praying in their hearts.
He commanded them to stand up on their feet. And they stood up.
He broke bread again, blessed it, and gave it to the disciples to eat.
When they had eaten, he commanded them to break bread and give it to the crowd.
When they had given bread to the crowd, he also gave them wine to drink and commanded them to give it to the crowd.
Now, the disciples had not brought any bread or wine, and neither had the crowd.
But he truly gave them bread to eat and wine to drink.
He said to them: Whoever eats this bread eats of my body for their soul, and whoever drinks this wine drinks of my blood for their soul. Their soul shall never hunger or thirst, but shall be filled.
When the entire crowd had eaten and drunk, they were filled with the Spirit. They cried out with one voice and gave glory to Jesus, whom they both saw and heard.
When they had all given glory to Jesus, he said to them: Now I will finish the commandment the Father has given me concerning this people, who are a remnant of the house of Israel.
You remember that I told you that when the words of Isaiah are fulfilled—and they are written down, you have them before you, so search them—
Truly I say to you, when those words are fulfilled, then the covenant the Father made with his people, O house of Israel, will be fulfilled.

Then the remnants who have been scattered across the face of the earth will be gathered from the east and from the west, from the south and from the north. They will be brought to the knowledge of the Lord their God, who has redeemed them.
The Father has commanded me to give you this land for your inheritance.
I say to you that if the Gentiles do not repent after the blessing they receive, after they have scattered my people—

Then you, who are a remnant of the house of Jacob, will go among them. You will be among many people, and you will be among them like a lion among the animals of the forest, or like a young lion among flocks of sheep—who, if he passes through, tramples and tears in pieces, and no one can rescue them.
Your hand will be raised against your enemies, and all your enemies will be destroyed.
I will gather my people together as a man gathers his sheaves to the threshing floor.

For I will make my people—with whom the Father has made a covenant—I will make your horn iron and your hoofs bronze. You will crush many peoples, and I will consecrate what they gain to the Lord, and their wealth to the Lord of the whole earth. I am the one who does this.
It shall come to pass, says the Father, that the sword of my justice will hang over them on that day. Unless they repent, it will fall upon them, says the Father—upon all the nations of the Gentiles.
I will establish my people, O house of Israel.

I will establish this people in this land, to fulfill the covenant I made with your father Jacob. It shall be a New Jerusalem. The powers of heaven will be among this people—I myself will be among you.

I am the one Moses spoke of when he said: 'The Lord your God will raise up a prophet for you from among your own people, like me. You must listen to everything he tells you.' And every person who will not listen to that prophet will be cut off from among the people.
Truly I say to you, all the prophets from Samuel onward—as many as have spoken—have testified of me.

You are the children of the prophets. You are of the house of Israel. You are part of the covenant the Father made with your ancestors, when he said to Abraham: 'Through your descendants, all the families of the earth will be blessed.'
The Father raised me up and sent me to you first, to bless you by turning each of you away from your sins—because you are the children of the covenant.

After you were blessed, the Father fulfills the covenant he made with Abraham, saying: 'Through your descendants, all the families of the earth will be blessed'—through the pouring out of the Holy Ghost through me upon the Gentiles. This blessing upon the Gentiles will make them powerful above all others, even to the scattering of my people, O house of Israel.

The Gentiles will be a trial to the people of this land. Nevertheless, after they have received my gospel in its fullness, if they harden their hearts against me, I will return their sins upon their own heads, says the Father.

I will remember the covenant I have made with my people. I have promised that I would gather them together in my own time and give them again the land of their ancestors as their inheritance—the land of Jerusalem, which is the promised land to them forever, says the Father.
The time will come when my gospel in its fullness will be preached to them.
They will believe in me—that I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God—and will pray to the Father in my name.
Then their watchmen will lift up their voices, and together they will sing, for they will see eye to eye.
Then the Father will gather them together again and give them Jerusalem as the land of their inheritance.
Then they will break out in joy—Sing together, you ruined places of Jerusalem! For the Father has comforted his people; he has redeemed Jerusalem.
The Father has revealed his holy power in the sight of all nations, and all the ends of the earth will see the salvation of the Father. The Father and I are one.

Then what is written will come to pass: Awake, awake again, and put on your strength, O Zion! Put on your beautiful garments, O Jerusalem, the holy city, for from now on the unclean will no longer enter you.
Shake yourself from the dust; arise and sit down, O Jerusalem. Free yourself from the chains around your neck, O captive daughter of Zion.
For thus says the Lord: You sold yourselves for nothing, and you will be redeemed without money.
Truly I say to you, my people will know my name. In that day they will know that I am the one who speaks.

Then they will say: How beautiful upon the mountains are the feet of him who brings good news, who proclaims peace, who brings glad tidings of good things, who proclaims salvation, who says to Zion: Your God reigns!
Then a cry will go forth: Depart, depart! Go out from there! Do not touch what is unclean! Go out from the midst of her! Be clean, you who carry the vessels of the Lord.
For you will not leave in haste or flee in a rush, for the Lord will go before you, and the God of Israel will guard you from behind.
My servant will act wisely. He will be raised up and lifted high and be very exalted.
Just as many were astonished at him—his appearance was so disfigured, more than any man, and his form more than any human—
So shall he sprinkle many nations. Kings will shut their mouths before him, for what they had not been told they will see, and what they had not heard they will understand.

Truly I say to you, all these things will surely come to pass, just as the Father has commanded me. Then the covenant the Father has made with his people will be fulfilled, and Jerusalem will be inhabited again by my people, and it will be the land of their inheritance.

21.text: And truly I tell you, I give you a sign so that you may know when these things are about to happen -- when I will gather my people, O house of Israel, from their long scattering, and will establish my Zion among them again.

And this is the sign I will give you -- for truly I tell you that when these things I am telling you, and the things I will tell you later by my own power and by the power of the Holy Ghost given to you from the Father, are made known to the Gentiles so that they may learn about this people who are a remnant of the house of Jacob, and about my people who will be scattered by them --
Truly, truly, I tell you, when these things are made known to the Gentiles by the Father, and come forth from the Father through them to you --

For it is the Father's wisdom that the Gentiles should be established in this land and set up as a free people by the power of the Father, so that these things might come from them to a remnant of your descendants, so that the covenant the Father made with his people, O house of Israel, may be fulfilled.
Therefore, when these works and the works that will be done among you later come forth from the Gentiles to your descendants who will lose their faith because of wickedness --

For the Father has planned that it should come through the Gentiles, so that he may show his power to the Gentiles. Because of this, if the Gentiles will not harden their hearts, they may repent and come to me and be baptized in my name and learn the true points of my doctrine, so that they may be counted among my people, O house of Israel.

And when these things happen and your descendants begin to learn about them -- it will be a sign to them that they may know that the Father's work has already begun to fulfill the covenant he made with the people of the house of Israel.
And when that day comes, kings will shut their mouths, for they will see what they had not been told, and they will consider what they had not heard.

For in that day, for my sake the Father will do a work that will be a great and marvelous work among them; and there will be those among them who will not believe it, even though someone declares it to them.

But the life of my servant will be in my hand; therefore they will not hurt him, although he will be harmed because of them. Yet I will heal him, for I will show them that my wisdom is greater than the cunning of the devil.

Therefore whoever will not believe in my words -- I who am Jesus Christ -- which the Father will cause my servant to bring forth to the Gentiles, giving him power to bring them forth (it will be done just as Moses said), they will be cut off from among my covenant people.

And my people who are a remnant of Jacob will be among the Gentiles, yes, in their midst like a lion among the animals of the forest, like a young lion among flocks of sheep, who tramples and tears in pieces as he goes, and no one can rescue them.
Their hand will be raised against their enemies, and all their enemies will be cut off.
Yes, sorrow will come to the Gentiles unless they repent; for in that day, says the Father, I will remove your horses from among you and destroy your chariots.
And I will cut off the cities of your land and throw down all your fortresses.
And I will cut off witchcraft from your land, and you will have no more fortune-tellers.
I will also cut off your carved idols and your standing images from among you, and you will no longer worship the things your hands have made.
And I will tear down your sacred groves from among you; and I will destroy your cities.
And it will happen that all lying, deceiving, envying, conflict, corrupt religious practices, and immorality will be done away with.
For it will happen, says the Father, that on that day whoever will not repent and come to my Beloved Son, I will cut them off from among my people, O house of Israel.
And I will carry out vengeance and fury upon them, just as upon the unbelieving nations -- the kind they have never known.

But if they will repent and listen to my words and not harden their hearts, I will establish my church among them, and they will enter into the covenant and be counted among this remnant of Jacob, to whom I have given this land as their inheritance.
And they will help my people, the remnant of Jacob, and also as many of the house of Israel as will come, to build a city that will be called the New Jerusalem.
And then they will help my people who are scattered across the whole face of the land to be gathered into the New Jerusalem.
And then the power of heaven will come down among them; and I also will be in their midst.

And then the Father's work will begin on that day, when this gospel is preached among the remnant of this people. Truly I tell you, on that day the Father's work will begin among all the scattered of my people, yes, even the tribes that have been lost, which the Father led away from Jerusalem.
Yes, the work will begin among all the scattered of my people, with the Father preparing the way for them to come to me, so they may call on the Father in my name.
Yes, and then the work will begin, with the Father working among all nations to prepare the way for his people to be gathered home to the land of their inheritance.
And they will go out from all nations; and they will not go out in a hurry or in flight, for I will go before them, says the Father, and I will guard them from behind.

22.text: And then what is written will come to pass: Sing, O childless one, you who did not give birth; break out in singing and cry aloud, you who did not labor in childbirth; for the children of the lonely one are more than the children of the married wife, says the Lord.
Make the place of your tent larger, and let the curtains of your dwelling be stretched out; do not hold back -- lengthen your cords and strengthen your stakes.
For you will spread out to the right and to the left, and your descendants will inherit the Gentiles and will cause the desolate cities to be lived in again.

Do not be afraid, for you will not be put to shame; do not be embarrassed, for you will not be disgraced. You will forget the shame of your youth, and you will no longer remember the disgrace of your widowhood.
For your Maker is your husband -- the Lord of Hosts is his name; and your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel -- he will be called the God of the whole earth.
For the Lord has called you back like a woman who was abandoned and deeply grieved in spirit, like a young wife who was rejected, says your God.
For a brief moment I left you, but with great mercy I will gather you.
In a moment of anger I hid my face from you, but with everlasting kindness I will have mercy on you, says the Lord your Redeemer.
For this is like the waters of Noah to me, for just as I swore that the waters of Noah would never again cover the earth, so I have sworn that I will not be angry with you.
For the mountains may depart and the hills may be removed, but my kindness will not depart from you, and my covenant of peace will not be taken away, says the Lord who has mercy on you.
O you who are afflicted, tossed by storms, and not comforted! I will set your stones with beautiful colors and lay your foundations with sapphires.
And I will make your windows of agates, and your gates of precious stones, and all your borders of beautiful stones.
And all your children will be taught by the Lord, and great will be the peace of your children.
In righteousness you will be established; you will be far from oppression, for you will not be afraid, and far from terror, for it will not come near you.
If anyone gathers together against you, it will not be by my doing; whoever gathers against you will fall because of you.
I have created the blacksmith who fans the coals in the fire and makes tools for his work; and I have created the one who destroys.

No weapon made against you will succeed; and every tongue that speaks against you in judgment you will prove wrong. This is the heritage of the servants of the Lord, and their righteousness comes from me, says the Lord.

23.text: And now, I tell you that you should search these things. Yes, I give you a commandment to search these things carefully, for great are the words of Isaiah.
For surely he spoke about all things concerning my people who are of the house of Israel; therefore he must also speak to the Gentiles.
And everything he spoke has been and will be fulfilled, just as he said.
Therefore pay attention to my words; write the things I have told you, and according to the timing and the will of the Father they will go forth to the Gentiles.
And whoever will listen to my words and repent and be baptized will be saved. Search the prophets, for there are many who testify of these things.
And now when Jesus had said these things, after he had explained all the scriptures they had received, he said to them: I have other scriptures that I want you to write down, which you do not have.
And he said to Nephi: Bring out the record you have kept.
And when Nephi had brought out the records and placed them before him, Jesus looked at them and said:

Truly I tell you, I commanded my servant Samuel the Lamanite to testify to this people that on the day the Father would glorify his name in me, many saints would rise from the dead and appear to many people and minister to them. And he said to them: Was it not so?
And his disciples answered him and said: Yes, Lord, Samuel did prophesy according to your words, and they were all fulfilled.
And Jesus said to them: How is it that you have not written this -- that many saints rose from the dead and appeared to many and ministered to them?
And Nephi remembered that this had not been written.
And Jesus commanded that it should be written; so it was written as he commanded.
And now when Jesus had explained all the scriptures together that they had written, he commanded them to teach the things he had explained to them.

24.text: And he commanded them to write the words that the Father had given to Malachi, which he would tell them. And after they were written, he explained them. And these are the words he told them, saying: Thus said the Father to Malachi -- I will send my messenger, and he will prepare the way before me, and the Lord whom you seek will suddenly come to his temple, the messenger of the covenant in whom you delight; he will come, says the Lord of Hosts.
But who can endure the day of his coming, and who can stand when he appears? For he is like a refiner's fire and like a cleaner's soap.
And he will sit as a refiner and purifier of silver; and he will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and silver, so that they may offer to the Lord an offering in righteousness.
Then the offering of Judah and Jerusalem will be pleasing to the Lord, as in ancient days and in years past.

And I will come near to you for judgment; and I will be a swift witness against those who practice sorcery, against those who commit adultery, against those who swear falsely, against those who cheat workers of their wages, who oppress widows and the fatherless, who push aside the foreigner, and who do not fear me, says the Lord of Hosts.
For I am the Lord; I do not change. Therefore you sons of Jacob have not been destroyed.
From the days of your ancestors you have turned away from my commandments and have not kept them. Return to me, and I will return to you, says the Lord of Hosts. But you say: How should we return?
Will a man rob God? Yet you have robbed me. But you say: How have we robbed you? In tithes and offerings.
You are cursed with a curse, for you have robbed me -- this whole nation has.

Bring all the tithes into the storehouse, so that there may be food in my house; and test me now in this, says the Lord of Hosts, and see if I will not open the windows of heaven and pour out a blessing so great that there will not be room enough to receive it.
And I will hold back the destroyer for your sake, and he will not ruin the crops of your land; and your vines in the field will not fail to produce fruit, says the Lord of Hosts.
And all nations will call you blessed, for you will be a land of delight, says the Lord of Hosts.
Your words have been harsh against me, says the Lord. Yet you say: What have we spoken against you?
You have said: It is useless to serve God. What have we gained by keeping his commandments and walking around mournfully before the Lord of Hosts?
And now we consider the proud to be happy; yes, those who do wickedly prosper; yes, those who test God even escape punishment.

Then those who feared the Lord spoke with one another often, and the Lord listened and heard them; and a book of remembrance was written before him for those who feared the Lord and thought about his name.
And they will be mine, says the Lord of Hosts, on that day when I gather my treasured ones; and I will spare them as a man spares his own son who serves him.
Then you will return and see the difference between the righteous and the wicked, between those who serve God and those who do not.

25.text: For the day is coming that will burn like an oven; and all the proud, and all who do wickedly, will be like dry straw; and the coming day will burn them up, says the Lord of Hosts, leaving them neither root nor branch.
But for you who fear my name, the Son of Righteousness will rise with healing in his wings; and you will go out and leap like calves released from the stall.
And you will trample the wicked; for they will be ashes under the soles of your feet on the day that I do this, says the Lord of Hosts.
Remember the law of Moses, my servant, which I commanded him at Horeb for all Israel, with its rules and judgments.
I will send you Elijah the prophet before the coming of the great and dreadful day of the Lord.
And he will turn the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the hearts of the children to their fathers, so that I do not come and strike the earth with a curse.

26.text: And now when Jesus had spoken these things, he explained them to the multitude; and he explained all things to them, both great and small.
And he said: These scriptures, which you did not have, the Father commanded that I should give to you; for it was wisdom in him that they should be given to future generations.

And he explained all things, from the beginning until the time he would come in his glory -- even all things that would happen on the earth, until the elements melt with intense heat, and the earth is rolled up like a scroll, and the heavens and the earth pass away.
And even to the great and last day, when all people, all families, and all nations and languages will stand before God to be judged by their works, whether they are good or evil --

If good, to the resurrection of everlasting life; and if evil, to the resurrection of damnation -- the two side by side, one on one hand and the other on the other, according to the mercy, justice, and holiness that is in Christ, who existed before the world began.
And now not even a hundredth part of the things Jesus truly taught the people could be written in this book.
But the plates of Nephi contain the greater part of the things he taught the people.

And I have written these things, which are a smaller part of what he taught the people; and I have written them so that they may be brought again to this people through the Gentiles, according to what Jesus said.
And when the Gentiles receive this, which they should have first to test their faith, and if they believe these things, then the greater things will be made known to them.
And if they will not believe these things, then the greater things will be kept from them, to their condemnation.
I was about to write everything that was engraved on the plates of Nephi, but the Lord told me not to, saying: I will test the faith of my people.
Therefore I, Mormon, write the things the Lord has commanded me. And now I, Mormon, end my own words and go on to write the things I have been commanded to write.
Therefore, I want you to know that the Lord truly did teach the people for three days; and after that he showed himself to them many times, and often broke bread and blessed it and gave it to them.

And he taught and ministered to the children of the multitude who have been mentioned, and he loosened their tongues, and they spoke to their parents great and marvelous things, even greater than what Jesus had revealed to the people; and he loosened their tongues so they could speak.

And after he had ascended into heaven -- the second time he showed himself to them, and had gone to the Father after healing all their sick, their lame, opening the eyes of their blind, unstopping the ears of the deaf, doing all kinds of miracles among them, and raising a man from the dead, and showing his power to them, and ascending to the Father --

The next day the multitude gathered together, and they both saw and heard these children; even babies opened their mouths and spoke marvelous things; and it was forbidden for anyone to write what they said.
And the disciples Jesus had chosen began from that time to baptize and teach everyone who came to them; and all who were baptized in the name of Jesus were filled with the Holy Ghost.
And many of them saw and heard things beyond words, which were not permitted to be written.
And they taught and ministered to one another; and they had all things in common among them, every person dealing justly with one another.
And they did all things just as Jesus had commanded them.
And those who were baptized in the name of Jesus were called the church of Christ.

27.text: And as the disciples of Jesus were traveling and preaching the things they had both heard and seen, and were baptizing in the name of Jesus, the disciples gathered together and united in mighty prayer and fasting.
And Jesus again showed himself to them, for they were praying to the Father in his name; and Jesus came and stood among them and said: What do you want me to give you?
And they said to him: Lord, we want you to tell us the name by which we should call this church; for there are disagreements among the people about this.
And the Lord said to them: Truly, truly, I tell you, why do the people complain and argue about this?
Have they not read the scriptures, which say you must take upon you the name of Christ, which is my name? For by this name you will be called at the last day.
And whoever takes upon them my name and endures to the end will be saved at the last day.
Therefore, whatever you do, you must do it in my name; therefore you must call the church by my name; and you must call upon the Father in my name so that he will bless the church for my sake.

And how can it be my church unless it is called by my name? For if a church is called by Moses' name, then it is Moses' church; or if it is called by the name of a man, then it is the church of a man; but if it is called by my name, then it is my church, if it is built upon my gospel.

Truly I tell you, you are built upon my gospel; therefore you must call everything you do by my name; therefore if you call upon the Father for the church, and it is in my name, the Father will hear you.
And if the church is built upon my gospel, then the Father will show forth his own works in it.

But if it is not built upon my gospel, and is built upon the works of men or the works of the devil, truly I tell you, they will have joy in their works for a time, but eventually the end comes, and they are cut down and thrown into the fire, from which there is no return.
For their works follow them; it is because of their works that they are cut down. Therefore remember the things I have told you.
I have given you my gospel, and this is the gospel I have given you -- that I came into the world to do the will of my Father, because my Father sent me.

And my Father sent me so that I might be lifted up on the cross; and after I was lifted up on the cross, I would draw all people to me, so that just as I was lifted up by men, people would also be lifted up by the Father to stand before me and be judged by their works, whether good or evil.
And for this reason I was lifted up; therefore, according to the power of the Father, I will draw all people to me, so that they may be judged according to their works.
And whoever repents and is baptized in my name will be filled; and if they endure to the end, I will hold them guiltless before my Father on that day when I stand to judge the world.
And whoever does not endure to the end is the one who is cut down and thrown into the fire, from which they can never return, because of the justice of the Father.
And this is the word he has given to all people. And because of this he fulfills the words he has given, and he does not lie, but fulfills all his words.

And no unclean thing can enter his kingdom; therefore nothing enters his rest except those who have washed their garments in my blood, because of their faith, their repentance of all their sins, and their faithfulness to the end.

Now this is the commandment: Repent, all you people of the earth, and come to me and be baptized in my name, so that you may be made holy by receiving the Holy Ghost, and may stand spotless before me at the last day.
Truly, truly, I tell you, this is my gospel; and you know the things you must do in my church; for the works you have seen me do, you must also do; what you have seen me do, that is what you must do.
Therefore, if you do these things, blessed are you, for you will be lifted up at the last day.
Write the things you have seen and heard, except those things that are forbidden.
Write the works of this people that will come, just as what has already happened has been written.
For out of the books that have been written and that will be written, this people will be judged, for by those books their works will be made known to people.
And all things are written by the Father; therefore from the books that will be written, the world will be judged.
And know that you will be judges of this people, according to the judgment I will give you, which will be just. Therefore, what kind of people should you be? Truly I tell you, even as I am.
And now I go to the Father. And truly I tell you, whatever you ask the Father in my name will be given to you.
Therefore ask, and you will receive; knock, and it will be opened to you; for whoever asks, receives; and to whoever knocks, it will be opened.

And now my joy is great, even to overflowing, because of you and also this generation; yes, even the Father rejoices, and so do all the holy angels, because of you and this generation; for none of them are lost.
I want you to understand; for I mean those who are now alive in this generation; and none of them are lost; and in them I have a fulness of joy.

But it grieves me because of the fourth generation from this generation, for they will be led away captive by the devil just as the son of perdition was; for they will sell me for silver and gold, and for things that moths can eat and thieves can steal. And on that day I will visit them by turning their own works upon their heads.

And when Jesus had finished these sayings, he said to his disciples: Enter through the narrow gate; for the gate is narrow and the way is difficult that leads to life, and few find it; but the gate is wide and the road is broad that leads to death, and many travel it, until the night comes when no one can work.

28.text: And when Jesus had said these words, he spoke to his disciples one by one, saying: What do you desire from me after I have gone to the Father?
And they all spoke, except three, saying: We want that after we have lived a full life, our ministry that you have called us to may come to an end, so that we may come quickly to you in your kingdom.
And he said to them: Blessed are you because you desired this from me; therefore, after you are seventy-two years old, you will come to me in my kingdom; and with me you will find rest.
And when he had spoken to them, he turned to the three and said: What do you want me to do for you when I have gone to the Father?
And they were sorrowful in their hearts, for they did not dare to tell him what they wanted.
And he said to them: I know your thoughts, and you have desired the same thing that John, my beloved disciple who was with me in my ministry before I was lifted up by the Jews, desired from me.

Therefore, you are more blessed, for you will never taste death; but you will live to see all the doings of the Father among the children of men, until all things are fulfilled according to the will of the Father, when I come in my glory with the powers of heaven.

And you will never suffer the pains of death; but when I come in my glory, you will be changed in the twinkling of an eye from mortality to immortality; and then you will be blessed in the kingdom of my Father.

And again, you will not have pain while you live in the flesh, nor sorrow except for the sins of the world; and all this I will do because of what you have desired from me, for you have desired to bring the souls of people to me as long as the world stands.

And because of this, you will have a fulness of joy; and you will sit down in the kingdom of my Father; yes, your joy will be full, even as the Father has given me a fulness of joy; and you will be like me, and I am like the Father; and the Father and I are one.
And the Holy Ghost testifies of the Father and of me; and the Father gives the Holy Ghost to the children of men because of me.
And when Jesus had spoken these words, he touched each one of them with his finger, except the three who were to remain, and then he departed.
And the heavens were opened, and they were caught up into heaven, and saw and heard things beyond words.
And it was forbidden for them to speak about it; and they were not given the ability to express the things they saw and heard.

And whether they were in the body or out of the body, they could not tell; for it seemed to them like a transfiguration, as though they were changed from this body of flesh into an immortal state, so that they could see the things of God.
But afterward they again ministered on the earth; nevertheless, they did not speak of what they had heard and seen, because of the commandment given them in heaven.
And now, whether they were mortal or immortal from the day of their transfiguration, I do not know.

But this much I know, according to the record that was given -- they went out across the land and ministered to all the people, bringing as many into the church as would believe their preaching, baptizing them; and all who were baptized received the Holy Ghost.
And they were thrown into prison by those who did not belong to the church. But the prisons could not hold them, for they were split apart.

And they were thrown into the ground; but they struck the earth with the word of God, and by his power they were delivered from the depths of the earth; and so no one could dig pits deep enough to hold them.
And three times they were thrown into a furnace and were not harmed.
And twice they were thrown into a den of wild animals; and they played with the animals like a child plays with a lamb, and were not harmed.

And so they went among all the people of Nephi and preached the gospel of Christ to everyone on the face of the land; and the people were converted to the Lord and united with the church of Christ, and the people of that generation were blessed, according to the word of Jesus.
And now I, Mormon, stop speaking about these things for a time.
I was about to write the names of those who would never taste death, but the Lord told me not to; therefore I do not write them, for they are hidden from the world.
But I have seen them, and they have ministered to me.
And they will be among the Gentiles, and the Gentiles will not know who they are.
They will also be among the Jews, and the Jews will not know who they are.

And when the Lord sees fit in his wisdom, they will minister to all the scattered tribes of Israel, and to all nations, families, languages, and peoples, and will bring many souls out of them to Jesus, so that their desire may be fulfilled, and also because of the convincing power of God that is in them.
And they are like the angels of God, and if they pray to the Father in the name of Jesus, they can show themselves to whoever they choose.
Therefore great and marvelous works will be done by them before the great and coming day when all people must surely stand before the judgment seat of Christ.
Yes, even among the Gentiles a great and marvelous work will be done by them before that judgment day.
And if you had all the scriptures that describe all the marvelous works of Christ, you would, according to the words of Christ, know that these things must surely happen.

And sorrow will come to those who will not listen to the words of Jesus, and also to those he has chosen and sent among them; for whoever does not receive the words of Jesus and the words of those he has sent does not receive him; and therefore he will not receive them at the last day.
And it would be better for them if they had not been born. For do you think you can escape the justice of an offended God, who has been trampled under the feet of men so that salvation might come?
And now as I spoke about those whom the Lord has chosen -- the three who were caught up into the heavens -- I did not know whether they were cleansed from mortality to immortality.
But since I wrote, I have asked the Lord, and he has revealed to me that a change had to be made in their bodies, or else they would have to taste death.
Therefore, so that they would not taste death, a change was made in their bodies so that they would not suffer pain or sorrow, except for the sins of the world.

Now this change was not the same as the one that will happen at the last day; but a change was made in them so that Satan could have no power over them and could not tempt them; and they were made holy in the flesh, so that the powers of the earth could not hold them.

And in this state they were to remain until the judgment day of Christ; and on that day they would receive a greater change and be received into the kingdom of the Father, to never leave again, but to dwell with God forever in the heavens.

29.text: And now I tell you that when the Lord sees fit in his wisdom for these words to come to the Gentiles according to his word, then you may know that the covenant the Father made with the children of Israel concerning their return to the lands of their inheritance is already beginning to be fulfilled.
And you may know that the words of the Lord spoken by the holy prophets will all be fulfilled; and you do not need to say that the Lord delays his coming to the children of Israel.
And you should not think in your hearts that the words that have been spoken are meaningless, for the Lord will remember his covenant that he made with his people of the house of Israel.

And when you see these words coming forth among you, you should no longer reject the Lord's works, for the sword of his justice is in his right hand; and if you reject his works on that day, he will cause it to overtake you quickly.
Sorrow will come to those who reject the Lord's works; yes, sorrow to those who deny the Christ and his works!

Yes, sorrow to those who deny the revelations of the Lord, and who say the Lord no longer works by revelation, or by prophecy, or by gifts, or by tongues, or by healings, or by the power of the Holy Ghost!

Yes, and sorrow to those who say on that day, to make money, that Jesus Christ can no longer perform miracles; for whoever does this will become like the son of perdition, for whom there was no mercy, according to the word of Christ!

Yes, and you must no longer mock, reject, or make fun of the Jews, or any of the remnant of the house of Israel; for the Lord remembers his covenant with them, and he will do for them what he has sworn.
Therefore do not think that you can turn the right hand of the Lord to the left, so that he cannot carry out judgment to fulfill the covenant he has made with the house of Israel.

30.text: Listen, O Gentiles, and hear the words of Jesus Christ, the Son of the living God, which he has commanded me to speak about you, for he commands me to write, saying:

Turn, all you Gentiles, from your wicked ways; and repent of your evil doings, your lying and deceiving, your immorality, your secret sins, your idol worship, your murders, your corrupt religious practices, your jealousy, your conflicts, and all your wickedness and sinful ways, and come to me and be baptized in my name, so that you may receive a forgiveness of your sins and be filled with the Holy Ghost, and be counted among my people who are of the house of Israel.
